## Summary

This May 2026 guide (published May 8, 2026 - among the most recent sources) provides concrete KPIs and timelines for topical authority that are missing from most cluster-pillar guides. The most useful element is the explicit timeline for small domain growth: "3-6 months for the first visible consolidation, 9-12 months to see domain effect." This timeline is critical for the cadence planning guide because it sets realistic expectations for founders who need to know when their content investment will compound.

The guide's "number of pillars" recommendations are precise and team-size calibrated: small SMB with 1 writer = 1-2 pillars (work them well before adding more); mid-sized SMB with team or external agency = 3-5 pillars; established company with editorial team = 5-10 pillars. The "mature pillar with 12 clusters generates more qualified traffic than 4 half-finished pillars with 3 clusters each" maxim directly supports the depth-over-breadth principle in the command brief.

The structural table is the most compact summary of pillar + cluster + support article architecture in the research set:
- Pillar page: 2,500-4,000 words, full coverage, 1 per topic.
- Cluster article: 1,000-1,500 words, deep dive on specific subtopic, 8-15 per pillar.
- Support article: 600-900 words, long-tail and specific questions, 10-20 per pillar.

This three-tier architecture (pillar + cluster + support articles) is richer than the two-tier (pillar + cluster) model in most other sources and worth encoding in the stinger's cluster-pillar guide.

## Key data points / quotes

- Timeline: "3-6 months for the first visible consolidation, 9-12 months to see domain effect."
- KPIs for topical authority: +30-50% typical organic growth in 6 months; 3-6 months to consolidate a cluster; 5+ AI Overview citations/month as a target.
- Solo writer recommendation: 1-2 pillars maximum. "Work them well before adding more."
- "A mature pillar with 12 clusters generates more qualified traffic than 4 half-finished pillars with 3 clusters each."
- Three-tier structure: Pillar (2,500-4,000 words) + Cluster (1,000-1,500 words, 8-15 per pillar) + Support articles (600-900 words, 10-20 per pillar).
- Content maintenance: "Each month, identify articles that have lost traffic, update outdated ones and add new questions appearing in Search Console."
- "Concentration beats volume" - a site with 25 well-connected articles can rank better than a generalist with 250 scattered articles.
- AI Overviews citations: "the top metric in 2026" for topical authority.

## Implications for stinger-forge

- The 3-6 month / 9-12 month growth timeline is essential for `guides/07-cadence-planning.md` - it sets realistic expectations and prevents founders from abandoning content programs too early.
- The three-tier architecture (pillar + cluster + support) is a more nuanced model than two-tier that the stinger's cluster map template should accommodate.
- "1-2 pillars for solo writer" is the clearest and most conservative recommendation for the stinger's audience - use this as the primary recommendation.
- The monthly refresh cycle (identify traffic drops, update, add Search Console questions) should be in the cadence planning guide as a maintenance ritual, not just a one-time setup.
- The "5+ AI Overview citations/month" KPI is a useful metric for the stinger to track topical authority progress beyond just rankings.
